title: Richard Zeckhauser on Office Hours
summary: Richard Zeckhauser, Frank P. Ramsey Professor of Political Economy, at Harvard Kennedy School, sits down with Aroop Mukharji (@aroopmukharji) to talk about everything from how to test your optimism to eating salmon to paltering, and saving the Air Force money.
body: Richard Zeckhauser, Frank P. Ramsey Professor of Political Economy, at Harvard Kennedy School, sits down with Aroop Mukharji (@aroopmukharji) to talk about everything from how to test your optimism to eating salmon to paltering, and saving the Air Force money. The latest from Belfer See more Gallery 1 of 4 Richard Zeckhauser: Is the Air Force About to Make a Big Mistake? 2 of 4 Richard Zeckhauser: Does Everybody Lie? 3 of 4 Richard Zeckhauser: The Optimism Jump Test 4 of 4 Richard Zeckhauser: Risk Assessment and Salmon Richard Zeckhauser is the Frank P. Ramsey Professor of Political Economy, Kennedy School, Harvard University. He graduated from Harvard College (summa cum laude) and received his Ph.D. there. He is an elected fellow of the Econometric Society, the Institute of Medicine (National Academy of Sciences), and the American Academy of Arts and Sciences. In 2014, he was named a Distinguished Fellow of the American Economic Association. His contributions to decision theory and behavioral economics include the concepts of quality-adjusted life years (QALYs), status quo bias, betrayal aversion, and ignorance (states of the world unknown) as a complement to the categories of risk and uncertainty. Many of his policy investigations explore ways to promote the health of human beings, to help markets work more effectively, and to foster informed and appropriate choices by individuals and government agencies. Zeckhauser has published over 300 articles. His recent coauthored books are The Patron's Payoff: Conspicuous Commissions in Italian Renaissance Art (2008), and Collaborative Governance: Private Roles for Public Goals (2011). Apart from academics, Zeckhauser is a Senior Principal at Equity Resource Investments, a real estate private equity firm. He has won multiple national championships in contract bridge. _list.source: Belfer Center for Science and International Affairs, Harvard Kennedy School

result.main_content_en: The article features an interview with Richard Zeckhauser, the Frank P. Ramsey Professor of Political Economy at Harvard Kennedy School. The conversation covers a wide range of his professional and personal interests, including behavioral economics concepts like testing optimism and 'paltering,' as well as practical policy advice such as saving the Air Force money. It highlights his academic background, his status as an elected fellow of several prestigious societies, and his key contributions to decision theory, including the development of quality-adjusted life years (QALYs), status quo bias, and betrayal aversion. His work focuses on improving human health, market efficiency, and informed decision-making by individuals and governments. Beyond academia, his roles as a real estate private equity principal and a national champion bridge player are noted.
result.main_content_zh: 本文是对哈佛大学肯尼迪学院政治经济学教授理查德·泽克豪泽的访谈介绍。访谈内容广泛，涵盖其专业领域及个人兴趣，包括行为经济学概念，如测试乐观情绪和“模糊欺骗”，以及为空军节省开支等实际政策建议。文章概述了他的学术背景，提及他当选计量经济学会、美国国家医学院和美国艺术与科学院院士的荣誉，并强调了他对决策理论的重要贡献，包括质量调整生命年、现状偏见和背叛厌恶等概念的提出。他的研究工作主要致力于改善人类健康、提升市场效率以及促进个人与政府机构做出明智决策。文章还提到了他在学术之外的成就，包括担任房地产私募股权公司的主要负责人以及多次获得全国桥牌冠军。
keywords_en: American Academy of Arts and Sciences
